refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To ensure atomicity and clarity, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refined FR focuses solely on the load phase, maintaining solution neutrality by specifying what must be achieved without prescribing how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, verifying that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination
Adjust equipment operational parameters to meet cycle time
Implement real-time monitoring and adaptive control of load phase
Enhance operator training and procedural adherence for load phase efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination

potential_sources: Interdependence between load phase and travel/dump phases if sequencing is not well isolated
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on sequencing and coordination within the load phase, which can be designed to be independent of other phases, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time target is achievable through process optimization alone
Operational policies and safety plans allow for process adjustments
Real-time monitoring and adaptive control may require additional system complexity not covered here
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time monitoring and adaptive control as a secondary DP to enhance responsiveness and robustness in meeting cycle time targets under variable conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system responsible for managing and optimizing operational processes, including load phase sequencing and coordination.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges real-time data and status updates relevant to load phase optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and task assignments related to load phase operations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external systems and modules for coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion with load phase optimization logic centralized here; minimal diffusion needed outside.
Contains some physical process elements but lacks full control over sequencing and coordination logic, causing diffusion.
Focuses on remote control aspects; load phase optimization logic would be diffused across other modules.
Primarily safety monitoring; load phase optimization is outside its core responsibility, leading to high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the load phase process sequencing and coordination DP due to its central role in operational process management and ability to encapsulate the optimization logic with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase coupling if not well managed.
Changes in load phase logic might require coordination with Safety Services for compliance, risking diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase. The DP logically decomposes the FR by focusing on process sequencing and coordination, which are key factors influencing cycle time performance. This mapping is clear and grounded in the acceptance criteria of meeting the p95 cycle time target under specified conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase coupling if not well managed.
Changes in load phase logic might require coordination with Safety Services for compliance, risking diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and centralizes the load phase optimization logic with a high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2), indicating effective packaging of the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, confirming that NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary. However, interface complexity and coordination with other modules pose diffusion risks that should be managed carefully.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measurement of cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies via the Safety Services interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit external interfaces defined for telemetry, tasking, and network communication that support the realization of the FR through the DP.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface protocols and data contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to minimize coupling and diffusion risks.
Establish coordination mechanisms or integration tests to ensure changes in load phase logic maintain compliance with safety policies and operational constraints.
Document monitoring and feedback processes within the NGHE Solution module to verify cycle time performance against acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mappings, high containment, low diffusion, and feasible verification. The DP 'Optimize load phase process sequencing and coordination' sufficiently decomposes the FR, and the NGHE Solution module is an appropriate IM boundary with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, the recommended revisions focus on interface definition and coordination rather than further decomposition. Therefore, no further sub-FRs are needed at this time.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface protocols and data contracts with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to minimize coupling and diffusion risks, and to establish coordination mechanisms ensuring compliance with safety policies.

assumptions: The current DP fully addresses the FR for cycle time optimization under specified conditions.
The NGHE Solution module can effectively contain the DP with manageable diffusion risks.
Verification methods for cycle time and safety compliance are sufficient and feasible.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity increasing coupling if not well managed.
Changes in load phase logic requiring coordination with Safety Services for compliance, risking diffusion.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface protocols and data contracts between NGHE Solution and external modules.
Integration test plans demonstrating coordination and compliance maintenance.
Monitoring and feedback process documentation within NGHE Solution module.
